comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report


Last Updated: 3/27/26 | 12:44PM

Good afternoon, Sunday River!

We certainly got our daily dose of sunshine this morning. Sure, it's on the chillier side with the breeze, but with clear skies, the sun is warming us right up. Lifts spin until 4PM today. Kickstart your weekend with some afternoon laps.

Looking ahead, we've got an action-packed weekend to get stoked for.

Over on Lollapalooza, the comps team is putting on their annual downhill race. From 8AM to around 11AM, that trail will be roped off from the top down to the Sliders deck. Access out of the hotel will still be available, but if you need to return there during that time the shuttle bus can take you there.

If you're a fan of hot dogs, good tunes, park laps, and free stuff you're going to want to head to Who-Ville from 1-3PM tomorrow for the Party in the Park. Hot Dogs are on a first-come, first-serve basis so don't wait to get over there.

Tomorrow is the last day for the White Heat Quad. If you're craving one last Heat lap for the year make sure to hit it then.

And the cherry on top for the weekend is Pond-A-Palooza on Sunday. We're extra stoked for that.

If you want in on all the fun, you can buy a 2026/27 New England Pass and start using it immediately with our Buy Now, Ski Now deal. Ski right away while also lining up next winter.

Bring the stoke this weekend.

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning from Sunday River,

We're kicking things off with a beautiful, sunny Friday morning at the 8 peaks. Currently, it is 23 degrees on Locke and 32 degrees down in White Cap. Temps dip slightly early this morning, then hold steady through the rest of the day, with highs topping out around 23 up top and 29 at the base. It's a windy start, with gusts pushing near 40 mph around 8AM, then easing back as the day goes on and settling closer to 30 mph by mid-afternoon.

Snow quality is right where you want it. We've picked up about 15 inches over the last week, and grooming has things running smooth and fast. Edges are holding, turns feel easy, and it's just one of those days where the mountain skis well top to bottom. First chair at 9AM is the move.

Quick heads up: Wonderland is closed today while we keep building out the pond for Sunday.

Looking ahead, this weekend is looking special.

Saturday, Who-Ville takes over with a full-on Party in the Park. Music, grills, merch giveaways, and plenty of features to send.

Sunday, Pond-A-Palooza goes down. Costumes, speed, and a very high chance of someone absolutely eating it in front of a cheering crowd.

Lastly, the lowest price on the 2026/27 New England Pass disappears on Thursday. After that, it's gone. Lock it in now.

We'll see you out there.

-Team Snow
